arts.ttl: Arsenal news: Emile Smith Rowe reveals 'toughest decision' before exit
arts.sum: Arsenal play Bournemouth on Saturday night - before Manchester City and Liverpool - meaning they have the chance to go top of the league temporarily and put pressure on their rivals

arts.ttl: Arsenal offered to sign £70m PSG star as White responds to 'hate' comments
arts.sum: So, with that being said, football.london has rounded up the latest Arsenal news and transfer gossip. According to TBR Football, Randal Kolo Muani has been offered to several Premier League clubs ahead of the January transfer window – one of which being Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Andoni Iraola provides positive Tyler Adams injury update ahead of Bournemouth's clash with Arsenal
arts.sum: Bournemouth head coach Andoni Iraola provides a positive update on Tyler Adams ahead of Saturday's Premier League home match against Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Raheem Sterling ready to play crucial Arsenal role after 'exceptional' training impact
arts.sum: Arsenal are about to embarked on a gruelling autumn schedule which will reveal much about their stomach for sustained title and Champions League challenges

arts.ttl: Arsenal favourite set to leave for exciting coaching role
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta has confirmed Under-18 manager Jack Wilshere has a decision to make on his future as he enters into talks with Norwich City. A move is expected to be finalised imminently, although Arteta suggested Wilshere's final decision has not yet been made.

arts.ttl: Ben White sets record straight on infamous claim about him - 'I just want to forget!'
arts.sum: A Sky Sports interview with White went viral shortly after he joined Arsenal from Brighton in 2021, with supporters left aghast when he claimed that he wasn't really a football fan

arts.ttl: Ben White sets record straight over whether he ‘hates’ football
arts.sum: Arsenal star White has previously made headlines for suggesting he never watches football once he leaves the training ground

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich chief fires cheeky Declan Rice transfer dig amid Arsenal admission
arts.sum: Bayern Munich chief Max Eberl has poked fun at Thomas Tuchel's failed pursuit of Arsenal star Declan Rice . This week, former Chelsea manager Tuchel was unveiled as England's manager which will see Rice play under the German tactician - nearly two years after he pushed for his services at club level.

arts.ttl: ‘Amazing’: Ben White says Arsenal have a ‘scary’ player who doesn’t get the credit he deserves
